acceptance_targets: [ch08-prose-001] PASS if no more than two of the cited lines still rely on a 'like' simile and the replacements convey concrete sensory or social consequence in Lena's viewpoint. FAIL if three or more cited lines retain the same simile scaffold.

[ch08-prose-002] PASS if the revised cited spans use the noun 'voice' in no more than two of these six locations and tone remains clear from surrounding action/dialogue form. FAIL if three or more 'voice'-framed attributions remain in the same spans.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the cited lines still rely on a 'like' simile and the replacements convey concrete sensory or social consequence in Lena's viewpoint. FAIL if three or more cited lines retain the same simile scaffold.
findings.problem: A recurring simile template (impact clause + 'like' comparison) appears repeatedly at high-pressure beats, flattening prose texture into a visible default move.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least three of the cited lines in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:83,153,155,191,365 by replacing the 'like' simile with direct sensory consequence or character-anchored observation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited spans use the noun 'voice' in no more than two of these six locations and tone remains clear from surrounding action/dialogue form. FAIL if three or more 'voice'-framed attributions remain in the same spans.
findings.problem: The repeated 'voice + adjective/action' attribution pattern appears well beyond twice and becomes a prose tic, reducing tonal variety during confrontations.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:99,111,209,233,251,309, rework at least four instances so tone is conveyed through syntax, interruption, breath, gesture, or microphone/crowd interaction instead of explicit 'voice' labeling.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
